title: AI recruitment startup Refer can help find you a job instead of stealing yours — but it will cost you
excerpt: Think of it as a virtual talent agent of sorts.
content_html:
Chris Morris

Sun, July 19, 2026 at 6:30 AM EDT 4 min read

Generally, when people are approached by recruiters about a possible new job, they don't have to worry about paying any sort of fee. That's typically covered by the company doing the hiring. But this is the age of artificial intelligence — and everything is different now.

A new startup called Refer approaches recruitment and job hunting in a novel manner, but it's one that puts the onus of payment on the job seeker, who will have to surrender 20% of their first month's salary (1) once they start the new gig.

Must Read

  • Jeff Bezos backs a platform that lets anyone invest in rental homes for as little as $100 — 6 ways to build wealth like a landlord without actually being one

  • Dave Ramsey warns nearly 50% of Americans are making 1 big Social Security mistake — here's what it is and 3 simple steps to fix it ASAP

  • Millionaires under 43 hold only 25% of their wealth in stocks. Here's where their money is actually going

Refer uses AI and handles the recruitment process in a mirror version of how it typically goes. Instead of working for the companies, its AI agent, which it calls Lia, acts on behalf of the job seeker. Think of it as a virtual talent agent of sorts.

You'll initially tell Lia what sort of job you're looking for, your experience, preferred salary and additional details. From there, it will search for potential matches and, if you give it permission, Lia will send an email to the hiring manager on your behalf.

Lia's track record

The system seems to be working. Refer's founder says more than half (2) of the service's users secure an interview within 24 hours of an introduction. To date, it has facilitated over 5,000 interviews.

Introductions come after both the job seeker and the potential employer express interest. Users can request for Lia to make up to five introductions per day. Employers then have three days to respond. The service bypasses traditional online applications entirely, saying they don't work.

If a candidate decides the job isn't for them, they explain why to the AI, which helps to train it to find more appropriate openings.

Refer works with approximately 2,000 employers and its current database spans 7,000 jobs, with a heavy emphasis on software engineering and other tech openings. That work, however, is handled by the humans on staff.

"While we use AI in our processes, our (human) team works hard to build and maintain relationships with decision makers at top tech companies, 'networking' on your behalf," the company writes on its website (1).

content_html_zh:
克里斯·莫里斯

2026 年 7 月 19 日星期日，美国东部时间上午 6:30 阅读 4 分钟

一般来说，当招聘人员联系人们寻找可能的新工作时，他们不必担心支付任何费用。 这通常由招聘公司承担。 但现在是人工智能时代——现在一切都不同了。

一家名为 Refer 的新初创公司以一种新颖的方式进行招聘和找工作，但这种方式将付款的责任推给了求职者，一旦他们开始新的工作，他们将不得不放弃第一个月工资的 20% (1)。

必读

  • 杰夫·贝索斯 (Jeff Bezos) 支持一个平台，让任何人都可以以低至 100 美元的价格投资出租房屋——无需真正成为房东即可像房东一样积累财富的 6 种方法
  • Dave Ramsey 警告说，近 50% 的美国人在社会保障方面犯了 1 个重大错误——这就是它的本质，以及尽快修复它的 3 个简单步骤
  • 43岁以下的百万富翁仅将其财富的25%投资于股票。 这就是他们的钱实际去向的地方

Refer 使用人工智能并按照通常的镜像版本处理招聘流程。 它的人工智能代理（称为 Lia）不是为这些公司工作，而是代表求职者行事。 将其视为某种虚拟人才代理。

您首先会告诉莉亚您正在寻找什么样的工作、您的经验、首选薪水和其他详细信息。 从那里，它将搜索潜在的匹配项，如果您给予许可，Lia 将代表您向招聘经理发送一封电子邮件。

莉亚的履历

该系统似乎正在运行。 Refer 的创始人表示，超过一半 (2) 的服务用户在介绍后 24 小时内获得了面试机会。 迄今为止，它已促成了 5,000 多次采访。

在求职者和潜在雇主都表达兴趣后进行介绍。 用户可以要求 Lia 每天最多进行五次介绍。 雇主有三天的时间做出回应。 该服务完全绕过传统的在线应用程序，称它们不起作用。

如果候选人认为这份工作不适合他们，他们会向人工智能解释原因，这有助于训练它找到更合适的空缺职位。

Refer 与大约 2,000 名雇主合作，其当前数据库涵盖 7,000 个职位，重点关注软件工程和其他技术职位空缺。 然而，这项工作是由工作人员处理的。

该公司在其网站 (1) 上写道：“虽然我们在流程中使用人工智能，但我们的（人类）团队努力与顶级科技公司的决策者建立和维持关系，代表您‘建立联系’。”

阅读更多：您是否为汽车保险支付了太多费用？ 这里有 3 种巧妙的方法来削减你的每月账单
